t: Adding your app’s content to Spotlight indexes
a: Create a description for your app’s content and add it to a Spotlight index to make it searchable.

t: Building a search interface for your app
a: Add a search interface to your app to execute Spotlight queries and offer suggested text completions.
A key that specifies the action’s identifier in a user activity.
A key associated with a custom attribute for a searchable item.
An object that provides searchable attributes for file types that the app supports.

A search source that retrieves indexed metadata from files and directories visible to Spotlight.

t: Generating summary and priority data for indexed items
a: Summarize mail, message, and audio transcripts or assess the priority of mail and messages using Spotlight and Apple Intelligence.

t: Making your indexed content available to Foundation Models
a: Make the content you index for Spotlight available to Foundation models to help generate responses to prompts.

t: Regenerating your app’s indexes on demand
a: Create an app extension to maintain your app’s indexes and regenerate them as needed.
